content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add to FileSystemLoader.__init__ a new optional parameter: fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]] = None (placed after searchpath, before or after encoding/followlinks - just keep it optional with default None so existing calls are unaffected). Process it exactly like searchpath is processed: if not isinstance(fallback_searchpath, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(fallback_searchpath, str): fallback_searchpath = [fallback_searchpath] if fallback_searchpath is not None else []; then self.fallback_searchpath = [os.fspath(p) for p in fallback_searchpath] if fallback_searchpath else []. Store as self.fallback_searchpath (a list, empty list when None provided).

In FileSystemLoader.get_source(): currently it loops 'for searchpath in self.searchpath' trying to open the file, returning on first match, else raising TemplateNotFound. Change so that after exhausting self.searchpath without success, it also loops 'for searchpath in self.fallback_searchpath' using the identical resolution logic (build filename via os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces), try open_if_exists, read/decode contents, compute mtime, build uptodate closure, return contents/filename/uptodate on first match). Only raise TemplateNotFound(template) if neither self.searchpath nor self.fallback_searchpath contain the template. Primary searchpath entries must always be tried before any fallback_searchpath entries, and the first match in searchpath wins even if the file also exists in a fallback path.

In FileSystemLoader.list_templates(): currently it walks self.searchpath dirs collecting template names into a 'found' set (skipping duplicates), then returns sorted(found). Change so it also walks self.fallback_searchpath directories with the same logic (os.walk with followlinks=self.followlinks, same path normalization stripping the searchpath prefix and converting os.path.sep to '/'), adding any newly discovered template names to the same 'found' set (so duplicates across primary/fallback are naturally deduplicated), and return sorted(found) covering both primary and fallback paths.

Backward compatibility requirement: when fallback_searchpath is None or not passed, self.fallback_searchpath is an empty list and get_source/list_templates behave identically to today (no behavior change, no extra iteration overhead beyond an empty loop).
note: fallback_searchpath feature for FileSystemLoader

Change so it also walks self.fallback_searchpath directories with the same logic (os.walk with followlinks=self.followlinks, same path normalization stripping the searchpath prefix and converting os.path.sep to '/'), adding any newly discovered template names to the same 'found' set (so duplicates across primary/fallback are naturally deduplicated), and return sorted(found) covering both primary and fallback paths.
